description_long: This gatehouse features two robust square towers constructed from reddish-brown stone blocks, each topped with crenellated battlements reminiscent of medieval fortifications. The central section is dominated by a large arched entryway framed in lighter stone, with a heavy wooden door reinforced by black iron hardware and a prominent latch. The structure sits on a broad stone base, and the towers are visually unified by a connecting parapet above the entry. The rear side of the gatehouse also has an arched doorway, but with a simpler wooden door and no visible ironwork. The overall design is imposing and defensive, with minimal window openings and a focus on solid masonry. The surface texture is rough and matte, emphasizing the historical and fortified character of the building.
description: A fortified stone gatehouse with twin crenellated towers, arched stone entryways, and heavy wooden doors reinforced with iron hardware.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main arched stone entryway with a reinforced wooden door and iron latch, flanked by two crenellated towers.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thickness of the stone walls and the depth of the towers, with no visible entry or windows.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a simpler arched wooden door without ironwork, and the same twin tower structure.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the solid stone wall and the profile of the towers, similar to view 1.
attribute_entryway_design: arched stone doorway with heavy wooden door and iron latch
attribute_window_placement: no visible windows, solid masonry throughout
